publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Energy Smart Systems (ESS)
abstract: Power active filters have become widespread as a tool for compensating power quality disturbances caused by non-linear and asymmetric loads. Based on the analysis of known studies of similar devices, it was established that the authors widely use the Fourier series expansion of the instantaneous power of an alternating current circuit. At the same time, the issues of energy redistribution in the elements of the power circuit have not been investigated. On the basis of the scheme of replacing the power circuit of the device, spectral modeling of the case of sinusoidal current of the network circuit was performed. It was established that the network phase power at the fundamental frequency is the sum of the inductance power and output power of the converter, and at the modulation frequency it is the difference, while the amplitude of the modulation components of the inductance and converter power is comparable to the amplitude of the main harmonic of the network power. The specified features should be taken into account when choosing the inductive elements of the power active filter.

title: Cyclical Temporal Encoding and Hybrid Deep Ensembles for Multistep Energy Forecasting
authors: Salim Khazem, Houssam Kanso
abstract: Accurate electricity consumption forecasting is essential for demand management and smart grid operations. This paper introduces a unified deep learning framework that integrates cyclical temporal encoding with hybrid LSTM-CNN architectures to enhance multistep energy forecasting. We systematically transform calendar-based attributes using sine-cosine encodings to preserve periodic structure, and evaluate their predictive relevance through correlation analysis. To exploit both long-term seasonal effects and short-term local patterns, we employ an ensemble model composed of an LSTM, a CNN, and a meta-learner of MLP regressors specialized for each forecast horizon. Using a one-year national consumption dataset, we conduct an extensive experimental study including ablation analyses (with/without cyclical encodings and calendar features) and comparisons with established baselines from the literature. Results demonstrate consistent improvements across all seven forecast horizons, with our hybrid model achieving lower RMSE and MAE than individual architectures and prior methods. These findings confirm the benefit of combining cyclical temporal representations with complementary deep learning structures. To our knowledge, this is the first work to jointly evaluate temporal encodings, calendar-based features, and hybrid ensemble architectures within a unified short-term energy forecasting framework. The code is available at https://github.com/salimkhazem/cai2025

title: A Measurement-Driven Framework of SISO FSA-FMCW Radar for High-Accuracy 2-D Localization
authors: Sijie Chen, You Lv, Xiaoying Zheng, Haojun Ge, Xiaonan Hui, Xianmin Zhang, Shilie Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Frequency-scanned antenna (FSA)-based frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radars provide a compact and hardware-efficient solution for 2-D localization by encoding both range and angle information into a single frequency sweep. However, practical FSA-FMCW systems often suffer from limited angular scanning caused by antenna open-stopband (OSB) effects and degraded direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation accuracy due to the mismatch between realized antenna responses and idealized models. To address these limitations, this article presents a measurement-driven framework for practical single-input single-output (SISO) FSA-FMCW localization. An OSB-suppressed substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) leaky-wave antenna (LWA) is employed to provide continuous frequency scanning over a wide angular range from −50° to 35° across 8–11 GHz. Based on measured far-field data, the complex equivalent array response is reconstructed under physical dispersion constraints, enabling signal-subspace-projection (SSP)-based DOA estimation that is robust to antenna-response mismatch and practical nonidealities. Outdoor experiments with the implemented SISO FSA-FMCW radar demonstrate high-accuracy 2-D localization, achieving an angular RMSE of 1.3° and a range RMSE of 11 cm. These results verify the effectiveness of the proposed framework for compact radar sensing platforms requiring accurate localization with low RF complexity.

title: A Dielectric-Modulated Dual-Material Gate MOS-HEMT for Ultra-High Sensitivity Label-Free Biosensor
authors: Hitesh Kumar, Kaushal Kumar Nigam, Chitrakant Sahu, Bandi Venkata Chandan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: This manuscript presents a comprehensive performance evaluation of a dielectric-modulated dual-material gate metal–oxide–semiconductor high electron mobility transistor (DMG MOS-HEMT) for various neutral and charged biomolecules incorporating source-side cavity to enable label-free detection. In this structure, dual-material gate electrodes with distinct work functions are employed to achieve a more uniform electric field distribution and to enhance electron velocity in the channel, thereby improving the device sensitivity. The neutral biomolecules are modeled by introducing a dielectric constant within the cavity, whereas charged biomolecules are simulated by varying the interface charge density at the oxide/AlGaN interface. The device structure is implemented and analyzed using the Silvaco ATLAS TCAD simulator. The performance of the device is evaluated by examining variations in drain current (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</sub>), threshold voltage (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</sub>), and channel conductance (g<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>). It has been observed that the proposed DMG MOS-HEMT exhibits superior sensitivity with respect to single-material gate (SMG) MOS-HEMT, with maximum changes of 256 mA/mm in I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</sub> and 2.0 V in V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</sub> for neutral biomolecules, and 76 mA/mm and 0.37 V, respectively, for charged biomolecules. Hence, these results substantiate the promise of DMG MOS-HEMT architecture for next-generation biosensing applications demanding ultra-high sensitivity.

title: HardVault: A Hybrid FPGA-Based Ethereum-Bitcoin Cold Wallet
authors: Joel Poncha Lemayian, Ghyslain Gagnon, Kaiwen Zhang, Pascal Giard
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Cryptographic wallets play a vital role in securing digital assets within blockchain networks by managing private keys that authorize secure transactions. However, side channel analysis (SCA) attacks have become a serious threat, enabling attackers to extract sensitive information by exploiting algorithmic weaknesses in microcontroller-based wallets, resulting in the loss of millions of dollars in digital assets. In hierarchically deterministic (HD) systems, the compromise of a single primary key can endanger all subsequent child keys, while the use of independent keys for each account introduces complexity and challenges in key management. This work presents HardVault, a field programmable gate array (FPGA)-based cryptocurrency wallet that supports both Bitcoin and Ethereum. HardVault introduces the first hardware wallet architecture that implements both non-deterministic (ND) and HD key generation modes directly in hardware, giving users the flexibility to choose either approach based on their security and usability needs. By leveraging constant-time operations and hardware-enforced private-key isolation, the design significantly improves resilience to SCA attacks. In addition, the architecture prioritizes resource efficiency to minimize area usage without compromising security, making it well-suited for compact, portable hardware wallet applications. Implementation on a ZCU104 FPGA shows that HardVault uses only 27% of available look-up tables (LUTs). Compared to the Trezor One cryptocurrency (crypto) wallet, the proposed implementation achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$9\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher energy efficiency, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> lower latency, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher throughput.

title: A Simultaneous Topology Optimization Method of Magnet and Iron-core for Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Based on Rectangular Shape Correction
authors: Yuju Sasabuchi, Takahiro Sato, Shinya Watanabe, Kei Nishihara, Kazuhiro Izui
abstract: This paper presents a topology optimization method for interior permanent magnet synchronous motors (IPMSMs), in which rectangular magnet arrangements and iron-core shapes are simultaneously optimized. In the present method, an image-processing-based rectangular correction scheme is integrated into the evolutionary topology optimization framework. This enables high-design-freedom exploration of rotor structures while automatically determining the magnetization directions of the rectangular magnets. The present method is applied to rotor design optimization of an IPMSM. In the symmetric design problem, the electromagnetic average torque is improved by approximately 5% compared with the baseline model without prior information on magnet layouts and magnetization directions. Furthermore, in the asymmetric design problem, various asymmetric magnet arrangements are discovered, and the average torque is improved by approximately 10%. Torque analysis based on the dq formulation considering cross-magnetization reveals that the asymmetric structures exploit torque components induced by cross-magnetization, which contributes to the torque improvement.

title: A Novel Method with Encoder-Decoder for Cross-Sensor Adaptation in Surface Shape Sensing with Sparse Strain Sensors
authors: Shuo Wang, Heng Luo, Dian Jin, Xiaoming Tao
abstract: Performance variations in sensor arrays, caused by intrinsic differences or installation conditions, can lead to inconsistent results during shape sensing. To obtain accurate results, a large amount of data is usually required, and a separate model must be retrained for each sensor array, thereby increasing the cost and time of data acquisition, transmission, and computation. To address this issue, this work proposes an encoder-decoder architecture for surface shape sensing based on sparse strain sensors and further incorporates meta-learning and few-shot adaptation strategies to enable adaptation across different groups of sensor arrays. Experimental results demonstrate that, after the cross-sensor adaptation, a newly deployed sensor array achieves a sensing error of approximately 4.0 mm relying on less than 5.0% newly labeled data and requiring an adaptation time of under 1 s, which represents a substantial improvement from 23.0 mm error without adaptation and 20-minute data collection time required to train a new model. Moreover, the number of points with errors below 5.0 mm increased by more than 65.0%. These results indicate that the proposed method can substantially reduce the cost and training burden of surface shape sensing, and it has broad potential applications in soft robotics and wearable devices.

title: AOTSS: Acoustic-optical Communication based Multi-AUV Collaborative Target Search Scheme via Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xiang Li, Peijun Dong, Hang Tao, Siyao He, Hanjiang Luo, Jiehan Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: In complex underwater environments, multiple autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) typically rely on under-water acoustic communication when performing collaborative target search tasks. However, traditional underwater acoustic technology has communication constraints (e.g., high latency and low bandwidth), which leads to poor information sharing and degrade the performance of multi-AUV collaboration target search missions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a multi-AUV collaborative acoustic-optical communication based target search scheme (AOTSS), which consists of two main components: a particle filter-based path planning algorithm (PFPPA) and a multi-agent reinforcement learning-based multi-AUV Collaborative Search Algorithm (MASA). In PFPPA, to implement efficient multimodal communication among AUVs, we design a navigation algorithm based on particle filter method and deep reinforcement learning. This approach maximizes optical communication to enhance information sharing among AUVs. Furthermore, In MASA we leverage multimodal communication to enhance information sharing among AUVs to obtain precise probability maps, and incorporate pheromones into these maps to guide AUVs performing efficient cooperate search via the Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MADDPG) approach. Through extensive simulations, the results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly enhances the multi-AUV collaboration target search efficiency.

title: RAHBAR: A RoBERTa-Augmented Hybrid biLSTM-biGRU Architecture for Intent Recognition in Education for Urdu Conversational Agent
authors: Muhammad Farrukh Shafeeq, Muhammad Aslam
abstract: The use of conversational agents in educational systems has drawn a lot of attention lately because of their potential to increase student involvement, decrease administrative burdens, and improve information availability. However, as most conversational agents are largely proficient in English, creating an intelligent conversational agent for low-resource languages like Urdu continues to be a significant issue. The absence of annotated datasets, the scarcity of pre-trained language models, and the subpar performance of current systems on rich datasets make it challenging to meet the needs of such low-resource languages. This study creates a domain-specific Urdu educational conversational agent with an emphasis on intent classification, a crucial element in comprehending Urdu queries, in order to address these issues. This paper presents the Urdu Intent classification Dataset (UID), a novel dataset of 11,598 Urdu queries and 268 hand-labeled intent classes that were gathered from actual users and were also validated against official university sources in order to address the lack of high-quality data. This study carried out a number of trials using numerous baseline models, including conventional machine learning models and sophisticated deep learning models, to assess the effectiveness of intent detection. Additionally, different transformer-based multilingual models were fine-tuned for performance. Furthermore, the study proposes a hybrid model called RoBERTa-Augmented Hybrid biLSTM-biGRU Architecture for Intent Recognition in Urdu Student Query conversational agent (RAHBAR), which combines a fine-tuned RoBERTa Urdu model with bi-LSTM, bi-GRU, an attention mechanism, and a transformer encoder for enhanced contextual understanding. The proposed model outperformed all state-of-the-art models, achieving 99% accuracy. Moreover, LIME was incorporated to enhance the explainability of the model’s predictions. This research presents a scalable, interpretable, and high-performing solution for Urdu intent classification, opening new avenues for conversational agent development in low-resource settings.

title: A Novel Reinforcement Learning-Based Multi-Objective Optimization Framework for Power Allocation in Multibeam Satellite Systems
authors: Dingxuan Fu, Benjamin K. Ng, Chan-Tong Lam
abstract: Multibeam satellite systems (MSS) are becoming a key component of future 6G non-terrestrial networks, providing wide-area coverage and high-throughput connectivity. However, their performance critically depends on power allocation, which leads to a high-dimensional and non-convex multi-objective optimization problem: the system must simultaneously minimize unmet system capacity and total transmit power. To address this challenge, this paper benchmarks representative multi-objective optimizers for MSS resource allocation using a simulation-based system model as a unified evaluator. Under static traffic demands, we systematically compare NSGA-II, MOEA/D, and multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO). Experimental results show that, under the same computational budget, MOPSO outperforms NSGA-II and MOEA/D in terms of hypervolume and inverted generational distance, establishing MOPSO as a strong baseline for MSS power allocation. Building on this baseline, we propose a reinforcement learning–basedMOPSO(RL-MOPSO), where a lightweight policy adaptively tunes swarm parameters based on online population statistics. We then evaluate RL-MOPSO in dynamic scenarios that emulate realistic channel conditions and demand variations. In these dynamic scenarios, RL-MOPSO achieves significant additional gains: it improves hypervolume by about 10%, reduces average USC by 40%, and lowers average transmit power by more than 90% compared with MOPSO. These results demonstrate that integrating multi-objective swarm optimization with reinforcement learning provides an effective approach for multibeam satellite systems power allocation.

title: BioSleepNet: Bio-Inspired Sleep Staging via Context Multimodal Fusion and Dynamic Temporal Memory
authors: Zhi Liu, Yunkai Gao, Yu Wu, Kangjia Tan, Guangdeng Li
abstract: Existing sleep staging research faces several challenges, including poor multimodal feature fusion, insufficient long-term dependency modeling, and weak adaptability to clinical staging logic. To address these issues, this study proposes an end-to-end multimodal sleep staging framework named BioSleepNet by leveraging the complementary characteristics of electroen-cephalogram (EEG) and electrooculogram (EOG) signals. Detailly, BioSleepNet incorporates three key modules. Multimodal feature fusion module performs differentiated feature extraction on EEG and EOG after Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) to raw signals, then fuses extracted feature with adaptive weight, to achieve efficient cross-modal feature collaboration. Contextual Temporal Feature module uses ModernTCN modeling contextual temporal feature to capture dynamic characteristics of sleep. And Momentum Memory module models long-term dependencies in temporal signals using a feature memory bank with attention-driven mechanisms, effectively capturing cross-cycle sleep continuity features.Considering the biological characteristics of difficult-to-model sleep transition rules and data imbalance across stages, we introduce a novel hybrid loss function named BioSleepLoss. BioSleepLoss significantly enhances the model’s adaptability to complex staging scenarios through learnable stage importance weights, dynamic difficulty-aware parameters, and adaptive transition probabilities. Experiments on the Sleep-EDF-20/78 datasets demonstrate overall accuracy of 89.3% and 87.1% respectively, with a Kappa coefficient of 0.85 and stage sensitivity standard deviation below 1.5%, verifying the robustness and clinical consistency of the proposed method. Ablation studies and feature visualization validate the effectiveness of multimodal feature fusion, contextual temporal feature modeling, momentum memory module, and physiological constraint collaborative optimization, which jointly provide an efficient and clinically cognizant solution for automated sleep staging.

title: Cyber-Physical Power System Dataset for Cyber Security of Digital Substations
authors: Nicola Cibin, Nadine Kabbara, Alfan Presekal, Ioannis Semertzis, Vetrivel Rajkumar, Himanshu Goyel, Peter Palensky, Alexandru Ştefanov
abstract: Cyber attacks targeting Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs) in digital substations can disrupt power system operation, causing equipment damage, instability, cascading failures, and even a blackout. Cyber-Physical Power System (CPPS) datasets are critically needed to develop novel methods for the detection and prevention of cyber attacks on digital substations. In this paper, a novel CPPS dataset is proposed for cyber security of digital substations, including real-time power system measurements, i.e., electromagnetic transient three-phase voltages and currents, communication network traffic, and virtual IED resource metrics. Various scenarios are simulated on an IEC 61850-compliant testbed consisting of Real-Time Digital Simulator (RTDS) and physical and virtual IEDs in hardware-in-the-loop configuration. The dataset contains different operating conditions and cyber attack scenarios, i.e., normal operation, single-phase-to-ground fault, network reconnaissance, resource exhaustion, and IEC 61850 Generic Object-Oriented Substation Event (GOOSE) and Sampled Values (SV) injection attacks. This work aims to provide the research community with a comprehensive and high-fidelity dataset to be used for the design and testing of novel methodologies to increase the cyber security of power grids.

title: UAV-Enabled ISAC with Fluid Antennas for Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Wenchao Liu, Xuhui Zhang, Jinke Ren, Weijie Yuan, Changsheng You, Shuangyang Li
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-enabled integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is regarded as a key enabler for next-generation wireless systems. However, conventional fixed-position antennas limit the ability of UAVs to fully exploit their inherent potential. To overcome this limitation, we propose a UAV-enabled ISAC framework equipped with fluid antennas (FAs), where the mobility of antenna elements introduces additional spatial degrees of freedom to simultaneously enhance communication and sensing performance. A multi-objective optimization problem is formulated to maximize the communication rates of multiple users while minimizing the Cram´er-Rao bound (CRB) for the angle estimation of a single target. Due to excessively frequent updates of FA positions may lead to response delay, a three-timescale optimization framework is developed to jointly optimize transmit beamforming, FA positions, and UAV trajectory based on their characteristics. To solve the non-convexity of the problem, an alternating optimization-based algorithm is developed to obtain a sub-optimal solution. Numerical results show that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms various benchmark schemes, validating the effectiveness of integrating the FA technology into the UAV-enabled ISAC systems.

title: CHORD: A Novel XAI Framework for Guiding Counterfactuals via Higher-Order Sensitivity Analysis
authors: Zohaib Shahid, Yogachandran Rahulamathavan, Safak Dogan
abstract: Building trustworthy AI systems requires robust and mechanically transparent explainability methods. Although counterfactual explanations effectively provide ‘what-if’ scenarios, their inability to reveal the underlying decision mechanisms in neural networks often limits their reliability. This paper introduces Counterfactuals via Higher-Order Relational Derivatives (CHORD), a novel framework that bridges the gap between sensitivity analysis and counterfactual generation. Unlike traditional first-order methods, CHORD utilizes higher-order partial derivatives to explicitly quantify feature interactions, mapping the complex non-linear dependencies that drive the outputs of a neural network. We demonstrate on the UCI Adult Income and UCI Bank Marketing datasets how this interaction-aware analysis can guide the generation of counterfactuals by targeting groups of coupled features, rather than isolated inputs. It was observed that using CHORD, if the value of the most impactful feature (based on the highest second-order partial derivative) is increased, a lower value of the most impactful feature (from the first-order sensitivity analysis, based on partial derivatives) is required to change the output. Crucially, our experiments show that prioritizing these interactions moves beyond simply finding a valid recourse to understanding the structural mechanisms that enable the switching of predictions. This synthesis results in explanations that are actionable, mechanistically interpretable, and mathematically grounded in the curvature of the network, thereby significantly enhancing AI transparency.

title: Adaptation and Evaluation of EEG Classifiers for Regression-based Hand Trajectory Decoding
authors: Miho Ohsaki, Minoru Inoue, Kimiaki Shirahama
abstract: One of the common techniques for Brain Computer Interface (BCI) is Electroencephalography (EEG). EEG is mainly used for classifying body parts or motor movements imagined by the brain. There are not many attempts to decode hand trajectories using EEG based on regression, since EEG signals are noisy due to their noninvasiveness. However, if such regression-based decoding is achieved using EEG, it could contribute to assisting physically disabled people in their daily lives with simple noninvasive devices. Toward this ultimate goal, our present study aims to adapt and evaluate recent representative EEG classifiers for regression-based hand trajectory decoding. We adapt EEG classifiers, EEG-TCNet, EEG-Conformer, and EEG-TCNTransformer from classification to regression. Specifically, we enable them to predict the 3D trajectories of a thumb and index finger given EEG time series as input. We then empirically evaluate them and the baseline CNN-LSTM, using the WAY-EEG-GAL dataset. As an experimental result, the mean squared error (smaller is better), the coefficient of determination (larger is better), and the correlation coefficient (larger is better) were obtained as follows: 0.653, 0.346, and 0.640 for CNN-LSTM. 0.500, 0.499, and 0.715 for EEG-TCNet. 0.810, 0.191, and 0.445 for EEG-Conformer. 0.635, 0.360, and 0.618 for EEG-TCNTransformer. EEG-TCNet substantially outperformed the other models across all evaluation metrics, whereas EEG-Conformer and EEG-TCNTransformer did not surpass the baseline CNN-LSTM. This present regression result, which was obtained under limited data and conditions, differed from the past classification result. Its generality should be carefully pursued using more data and optimized conditions in future work.

title: DFT-Based Spectral Precoding for Scalable Unique-Word OFDM
authors: Salil Sharma, Syed Waqas Haider Shah, Jesus O. Lacruz, Joerg Widmer
abstract: Unique-word orthogonal frequency division multi-plexing (UW-OFDM) addresses the spectral inefficiency of cyclic prefix (CP) OFDM by embedding the guard interval within the symbol period. However, its practical adoption is hindered by a prohibitive computational bottleneck. Conventional methods for embedding unique word within the DFT window rely on complex matrix inversions at the transmitter (Tx). While alternative zero-padded (ZP) schemes avoid this complexity, they place guard interval outside DFT window, breaking natural circularity and necessitating noise-enhancing Overlap-Add receiver (Rx). This letter proposes a scalable, closed-form isometric Spectral Precoding architecture that resolves this trade-off. By exploiting frequency-domain interpolation, we implicitly construct the required near-zero-energy tail within the DFT window using only deterministic linear transformations. We prove this construction reduces Tx complexity to log-linear order while preserving standard single-tap equalization without Rx noise enhancement. Validated with 5G-NR numerologies, the scheme matches CP-OFDM error performance while eliminating CP overhead.

title: A Robotic Simulation Environment for Ultrasound Imaging of Soft Tissue
authors: Jintan Zhang, Yixuan Wu, Adnan Munawar, Peter Kazanzides
abstract: Robot-assisted ultrasound imaging promises enhanced precision and consistency for diagnostic applications, but its development is hindered by the absence of integrated simulation platforms capable of modeling the complex interplay between robotic manipulation, soft tissue deformation, and acoustic wave propagation. Existing simulators lack integrated capabilities for both domains. To address this gap, we present a unified software pipeline that integrates Isaac Sim's FEMbased soft body simulation with k-Wave's acoustic simulation to enable physics-based ultrasound imaging of deformable tissues. Our key technical contribution is a novel data processing algorithm that maintains physically-plausible correspondence between deforming FEM meshes and heterogeneous acoustic property distributions through optimized spatial sampling. We validate our approach in simulation using complex digital patient phantoms with a Universal Robots-based ultrasound system, demonstrating physics-based B-mode imaging under 1 cm tissue compression. This work enables robotic ultrasound system prototyping, algorithm development, and synthetic data generation for machine learning.

title: Detection of Unknown Attack in Cyber-Physical Power Systems via Self-Supervised Cross-Layer and Cross-Temporal Consistency
authors: Luyu Wang, Kaifeng Zhang, Biplab Sikdar, Ying Wang, Wenjun Bi, Jia Ning
abstract: Modern power grids are increasingly exposed to diverse cyber-physical attacks that originate in the cyber layer and disrupt physical operation. A critical defense challenge is to rapidly determine whether an attack has occurred and identify its type. To address this challenge, this paper aims to develop a generalized detection scheme capable of identifying multiple types of attacks, particularly unknown ones. It is first observed that, under normal operation, cyber-layer data and physical-layer states are consistent across layers and evolve smoothly over time, while attacks disrupt these consistencies. Building on this insight, a self-supervised Cross-Layer Cross-Temporal Contrastive Learning (CL-CTCL) framework is proposed, which employs a Graph Attention Network-Gated Recurrent Unit (GAT-GRU) encoder to capture spatial dependencies and temporal dynamics. Two complementary contrastive objectives are introduced: Variance-Invariance-Covariance Regularization (VICReg) for cross-layer alignment and Information Noise-Contrastive Estimation with Momentum Contrast (InfoNCE-MoCo) for temporal consistency. Residual deviations from these consistencies provide reliable attack fingerprints, enabling detection and classification of both known and unknown attacks. Experiments on an IEEE 118-bus cyber-physical testbed show that the proposed CL-CTCL achieves high accuracy on known attacks and generalizes well to unknown ones, outperforming baseline methods across standard metrics.

title: Hybrid Feature Fusion for Multimodal Biometric Recognition: A Novel CNN-Handcrafted Feature Approach for Face and Palm Datasets
authors: Rajesh Namdeo Patil, Pravin Sahebrao Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Device Intelligence, Computing and Communication Technologies (DICCT)
abstract: Multimodal biometric systems have attracted a high level of attention because of their improved security and reliability over unimodal ones. This paper proposes a novel hybrid feature extraction technique based on the combination of deep convolutional neural network (CNN) features with handcrafted features for multimodal biometric recognition by using face and palm. The proposed framework derives complementary features by two parallel streams for learning hierarchical representations and handcraft feature module containing Local Binary Patterns (LBP), Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG), and Scale-Invariant Feature Transform (SIFT) descriptors. Feature-level fusion is carried out by the help of canonical correlation analysis (CCA) and score-level fusion by weighted sum rule. The Features of the hybrid are classified with Support Vector Machine (SVM) using the radial basis function kernel. Experimental evaluation on publicly available datasets (FERET for face and PolyU Palmprint database) shows significant performance improvement and an overall recognition accuracy of 98.7%, equal error rate (EER) of 1.2% and false acceptance rate (FAR) of 0.8%. The proposed system is up to 3.2 - 5.8% accurate than existing techniques, which proves the effectiveness of fusing learned and engineered features for reliable biometric recognition.

title: Development and characterization of a coaxial six-path, low-inductance, low-jitter spark gap switch with two gas filling modes for high-current pulsed power applications
authors: Yongyong Zhang, Yueming Zhu, Lulu Deng, Xiaobing Zhang, Y. C. Francis Thio, Xiaoming Zheng, Wuhan Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: This paper presents the design, development, and characterization of a novel six-in-one spark gap switch for high-current pulsed power systems. The switch consists of six independently triggered spark gap channels arranged coaxially and operated in parallel, enabling a total discharge current of up to 300 kA with low system inductance. A trigger-electrode electrical triggering mechanism is employed to ensure synchronous operation across all channels. The switch features two gas filling modes (with Argon and Nitrogen), supporting a wide working voltage range from 5 kV to 20 kV. Experimental results demonstrate low jitter (<25 ns), a fast current rise time, and consistent performance across repeated shots. The overall inductance of the switch assembly is below 25 nH, making it suitable for high-density plasma applications such as coaxial plasma guns. This work provides a robust and scalable solution for high-current pulsed systems requiring high current, low inductance, and stable triggering performance.

title: TriPrune-HMA: A Large-Scale Heterogeneous Multi-Core LSTM Accelerator Based on Two-Stage Triangular Pruning
authors: Quanyou Li, Le Yu, Wenxin Li, Jin Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) is widely used in tasks such as stock prediction and sentiment analysis. By introducing additional parameters into Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), LSTM improves accuracy but also increases computational complexity and inference latency in large and deep models. To address these challenges in LSTM-based time series prediction, this paper proposes TriPrune-HMA, a Heterogeneous Multi-Core LSTM Accelerator based on a structured two-stage Triangular Pruning method. The approach adopts algorithm–hardware co-optimization to compress the model while enabling efficient hardware execution. Algorithmically, a two-stage triangular pruning method is introduced, performing block-wise triangular region selection and nested boundary refinement under hardware-aware constraints to generate compact sparsity patterns that reduce network parameters while maintaining prediction accuracy. Hardware-wise, TriPrune-HMA employs a CPU-FPGA heterogeneous architecture with a dedicated data transmission protocol and multiple parallel processing units (PUs) implemented in the FPGA executor. Combined with optimizations such as Fixed-16 bit-width representation, block-wise storage, and piecewise linear activation functions, it improves resource utilization and energy efficiency. Experiments demonstrate that, under latency-critical streaming inference settings, TriPrune-HMA reduces latency by over 3.3× compared with high-end CPUs and GPUs in latency-critical batch-1 inference scenarios, improves energy efficiency by more than 38.9×, and achieves a throughput of 988.7 GOP/s, thereby providing an efficient and scalable solution for temporal inference in edge computing scenarios.

title: Deep Learning-Based Drone Heading Estimation Using BiLSTM and Multi-Head Attention: A Comparative Study of Simulation and Real-World Flight Experiments
authors: Ruppikha Sree Shankar, Vatsal Siotia, Rani Oomman Panicker, R Manoj
abstract: Autonomous drone navigation commonly relies on magnetometer-based heading estimation, which becomes unreliable in environments affected by magnetic interference. This paper presents a system-level real-time heading estimation framework that predicts drone orientation directly from inertial measurement unit (IMU) data without relying on a compass The proposed approach integrates Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks with a Multi-Head Attention mechanism to model temporal dependencies in accelerometer and gyroscope signals. The proposed approach is deployed within a Mission Planner–ArduPilot environment, where IMU data is streamed from the flight controller to an external computation module via MAVLink communication. The model processes sequential sensor data in real time and transmits predicted heading values back to the flight controller, enabling compass-independent navigation in a closed-loop configuration. The system is evaluated using both simulation and real-world flight experiments under compass-disabled conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.082° and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 0.109° in simulation-based experiments, while maintaining an MAE of 0.0932° in real flight tests. The model maintains stable trajectory tracking across multiple flight maneuvers, including hovering, straight flight, and aggressive turns. These results indicate that the proposed framework provides accurate and consistent heading estimation for real-time UAV navigation without reliance on magnetometer inputs, demonstrating its effectiveness in practical compass-denied flight scenarios.

title: Estimation of State of Charge for Lithium Ion Batteries using Data Based Deep Learning Approach
authors: Affan Abbasi, Muhammad Khalid
abstract: In recent decades, urbanization and population growth have led to increased carbon emissions, exacerbating climate change and global warming. This trend has compelled the power industry to transition to alternative energy sources due to heightened fossil fuel consumption and decreasing availability. The utilization of renewable sources for power supply is recognized as a pivotal step toward achieving sustainable energy goals. These sources, of the likes of solar and wind, are variable in nature and do not provide constant dispensable power supply, hence the need for battery energy storage systems (BESS). BESS store surplus energy from these intermittent sources and provide this energy as backup supply in the time of need. In BESS, the state of charge (SoC) holds significant importance for optimizing charge and discharge schedules which directly affect the rate of dispatch of power. The SoC of the BESS depends directly or indirectly on factors such as voltage supply, current source, operational temperatures, and time of use. Therefore, it varies throughout the life cycle of the BESS, highlighting the need for accurate prediction. This study proposes a deep feed forward neural network (DNN) based SoC prediction model for a Li-ion battery. The input layer of the DNN consists of five nodes: voltage, current, temperature along with average current and voltage values. The battery is studied under a variety of temperature conditions in a controlled environment, and data is recorded for constant and variable ambient temperatures. The trained DNN model had an RMSE of 0.025% and showed an accuracy of 2.6% for testing phase.

title: Integrating Symbolic Tokenization, k-mers, and Vision Transformers for Molecular Representation in BloodBrain Barrier Prediction
authors: Hannah I. Da S. Marques, Giovanna A. P. Soares, Matheus Dalmolin, Raquel De M. Barbosa, Marcelo A. C. Fernandes
abstract: The prediction of blood–brain barrier (BBB) permeability is a central task in molecular property modeling due to the selective nature of this physiological interface. This work investigates a representation pipeline that integrates symbolic tokenization of SMILES sequences, k-mer co-occurrence matrices, and image-based encodings processed by a Vision Transformer (ViT). Each molecule is converted into a Molecule Image Representation (MIR), from which embedding vectors are extracted and used as input features for four supervised classifiers: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB), Random Forest (RF), and Logistic Regression (LR). The evaluation is conducted on the BBBP and B3DB datasets under a reproducible experimental protocol that includes fixed random seeds, standardized data partitions, and repeated executions. Across both datasets, SVM and XGB obtain the largest AUC values in the validation and test sets, while RF and LR produce lower values under the same conditions. The results indicate that the MIR–ViT pipeline produces embedding vectors that support discrimination between BBB+ and BBB− samples and that nonlinear classifiers achieve the highest performance within this framework.

title: ScGDCF: Graphical Deep Clustering with Fused Common Information for Single-cell RNA-seq Data
authors: Yao Dong, Kunyu Li, Yongfeng Dong, Ziyu Ren, Jiaxue Zhang, Yushan Hu, Xuekui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Unsupervised deep clustering plays a crucial role in analyzing single-cell RNA sequencing data (scRNA-seq) as it helps to identify potential cell types. However, most existing clustering methods face challenges in effectively fusing common information between feature and topological structure informa tion, and they may not perform well on the sparse data, which are common in single-cell analysis. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Graphical Deep Clustering with Fused Common Information method for scRNA-seq data, named scGDCF. This method can accurately segregate different cell types even in large and sparse scRNA-seq datasets. In scGDCF, we first introduce a sparse feature representation method that utilizes an adversarial loss function to address the sparsity problem in scRNA-seq data and improve the performance of the discriminator. Next, we design a mutual information extracting operator to deeply mine and fuse the common information from feature and topological structure data, thereby improving the clustering performance. Furthermore, we incorporate the varying degrees of contribution from different neighbor nodes and information sources. To handle this, we promote a dual adaptive attention mechanism that oper ates at both global and local levels. Finally, experiments on seven real-world datasets and two simulated datasets show scGDCF outperforms 17 state-of-the-art methods. We further extend the clustering results for visualization, analysis of gene differential expression and enrichment, showing scGDCF provides novel insights into cell developmental lineages and preserved inter cluster distances.

title: Research on the Problems of Restoring Heat Supply to the Reoccupied Cities of Donbas
authors: Sokolenko Kostiantyn, Yevhen Rudniev, Sokolenko Valeriy, Vitaliy Shvets
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Energy Smart Systems (ESS)
abstract: The article offers a comprehensive approach to solving the problem of restoring heat supply to the de-occupied cities of the Donbas. The liberation of the occupied cities of Donbas will pose many challenges to the functioning, recovery and sustainable development of the urban economy. As a working hypothesis, we assume the possibility that energy supplies, primarily natural gas, will be cut off in the Donbas region. This will mean a period of shock adaptation. It is determined that the scale of destruction of urban infrastructure will not allow for a quick restoration of heat supply to the most destroyed cities. The problem of defining the concept of restoring heat and power supply systems in the de-occupied cities is studied. The article analyses the problems of sustainable operation of the electricity supply system in cities and towns during the heating season. The most difficult challenges will be the absence of district heating systems, overloading of power grids, and low energy efficiency of buildings. The development of the concept of heat and power supply systems for cities should be based on specific indicators of population density, taking into account the share of solvent households (subscribers). The basic principles at the stage of developing a heating system concept should include energy efficiency, autonomy, diversification of energy sources, use of renewable energy, environmental friendliness, and sustainable development.

title: Ensuring Consistency of Large Language Model Outputs for Similar Prompts using Group Relative Policy Optimization
authors: Sonal Prabhune, Balaji Padmanabhan, Kaushik Dutta
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in business-critical domains such as finance, education, healthcare, and customer support, where users expect consistent and reliable recommendations. Yet LLMs often exhibit variability when prompts are phrased with minor differences, even when semantically equivalent. Such inconsistency undermines trust, complicates compliance, and disrupts user experience. While personalization is desirable in certain contexts, many enterprise scenarios, such as HR onboarding, customer support, or policy disclosure, require invariant information delivery regardless of phrasing or prior conversational history. Existing approaches, including retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and temperature tuning, improve factuality or reduce stochasticity, but cannot guarantee stability across equivalent prompts. In this paper, we propose a reinforcement learning framework based on Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to directly optimize for consistency. Unlike prior applications of GRPO, which have been limited to reasoning and code generation, we adapt GRPO to enforce the stability of information content across groups of semantically equivalent prompts. We introduce entropy-based helpfulness and stability rewards, treating prompt variants as groups and resetting conversational context to isolate phrasing effects. Experiments on investment and job recommendation tasks show that our GRPO-fine-tuned model reduces variability compared to the baseline LLM model. To our knowledge, this is a novel application of GRPO for aligning LLMs toward information consistency, reframing variability not as an acceptable feature of generative diversity, but as a correctable flaw in enterprise deployments.

title: Evaluating the Techno-Economic Viability of a Solar PV-Wind Turbine Hybrid System with Battery Storage for an Electric Vehicle Charging Station in Khobar, Saudi Arabia
authors: Ahmed S. AbdElrazek, Mohamed Soliman, Muhammad Khalid
abstract: The main aim of this investigation is to replicate and enhance a sustainable hybrid energy structure that combines solar photovoltaic, wind turbines, battery storage. The study employs the Homer simulation model to evaluate the scaling, cost, and control strategy of this hybrid power system. This work primarily focuses on determining the most efficient design for a renewable energy generation system architecture for a significant electric vehicle charging station. The hybrid power system is designed to meet an AC base load of 2424.25 kWh/day with peak consumption of 444 kW. The simulation results indicate that the optimized components and the cost of energy are at an optimal level and the optimal design in terms of renewable energy penetration.

title: Hybrid 3D Asset Retrieval via Contrastive Vision-Language Matching and Structured Prompt Parsing
authors: Ahmad Alkhaldi, Igli Balla, Rahma El Bouazzaoui, Tirthendu Prosad Chakravorty, Hergys Rexha, Sébastien Lafond, Tristan Klempka
abstract: Searching large repositories to retrieve 3D assets for simulation environments can be tedious, especially when dealing with complex metadata. On the other hand, retrieving relevant assets using natural language remains a challenge, as embedding-based approaches often fail to handle prompts with multiple constraints, while metadata-based methods are too rigid for abstract or semantic queries. This paper proposes a hybrid retrieval framework that combines a vision-language model to extract semantic features from asset images with a structured natural-language parser to map user text queries to retrievable attributes. The system employs a dual-path architecture that utilizes contrastive embeddings for semantic matching, while a fine-tuned Phi-3-Mini model extracts explicit attribute-value pairs for metadata filtering. The approach was evaluated on a custom dataset of 500 multi-view 3D assets with structured annotations. Experimental results showed that the hybrid method outperforms standalone retrieval baselines, increasing top retrieval success by more than 55 percent for complex multi-attribute queries. The proposed framework enhances 3D asset retrieval workflows in simulation environments where both accuracy and attribute control are crucial, paving the way for automated environment creation.

title: Technical and Economic Indicators of the Energy Node, Which Supports the Average Daily WPP Generating Power
authors: Viktor Denysov, Tetiana Nechaieva
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Energy Smart Systems (ESS)
abstract: An approach to modeling the joint operation of wind power plant with a built-in storage system is proposed, which ensures the maintenance of the average daily power supply capacity to the grid throughout the day. If it is impossible to provide the declared capacity at the expense of the wind power plant and accumulated electricity capacity, the reserve highly maneuverable capacity used. In the event of an excess of the total generating capacity of the power node, the capacity of the supplied to the grid electricity limited. Calculations of the hourly aggregate wind power plant capacity for the integrated power system of Ukraine for 365 days of 2019 have been carried out. The results of modeling the operation of a power node, that provides the average daily capacity wind power plant, presented. The results of modeling the optimal use of the power node, which includes wind power plant with a battery electric storage system and a reserve power plant, confirm the possibility of its stable operation as part of the integrated power system of Ukraine.

title: Unlocking Human Movement: Detailed Gait and Impact Assessment from Consumer Earable Devices
authors: Jake Stuchbury-Wass
publish: Proceedings of the 24th Annual International Conference on Mobile Systems, Applications and Services Companion
abstract: None

title: Poster: MoCas-HAR: Lightweight and Generalizable Multi-Sensor Human Activity Recognition via Cascaded Fusion and Moment-Morph Regularization
authors: Wang Chenlong, Zhuo Yan, Ding Wenbo, Chen Xinlei

title: Investigating the Impact of Speech Enhancement on Audio Deepfake Detection in Noisy Environments
authors: Angela Anacin, Shruti Kshirsagar, Anderson R. Avila
abstract: Logical Access (LA) attacks, also known as audio deepfake attacks, use Text-to-Speech (TTS) or Voice Conversion (VC) methods to generate spoofed speech data. This can represent a serious threat to Automatic Speaker Verification (ASV) systems, as intruders can use such attacks to bypass voice biometric security. In this study, we investigate the correlation between speech quality and the performance of audio spoofing detection systems (i.e., LA task). For that, the performance of two enhancement algorithms is evaluated based on two perceptual speech quality measures, namely Perceptual Evaluation of Speech Quality (PESQ) and Speech-to-Reverberation Modulation Ratio (SRMR), and in respect to their impact on a state-of-the-art audio spoofing detection system, known as Audio Anti-Spoofing using Integrated Spectro-Temporal Graph Attention Networks (AASIST). We adopted the LA dataset, provided in the ASVspoof 2019 Challenge, and corrupted its test set with different Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) levels, while leaving the training data untouched. Enhancement was applied to attenuate the detrimental effects of noisy speech, and the performances of two models, Speech Enhancement Generative Adversarial Network (SEGAN) and Metric-Optimized Generative Adversarial Network Plus (MetricGAN+), were compared. Although we expect that speech quality will correlate well with speech applications’ performance, it can also have as a side effect on downstream tasks if unwanted artifacts are introduced or relevant information is removed from the speech signal. Our results corroborate with this hypothesis, as we found that the enhancement algorithm leading to the highest speech quality scores, MetricGAN+, provided the lowest Equal Error Rate (EER) on the audio spoofing detection task, whereas the enhancement method with the lowest speech quality scores, SEGAN, led to the lowest EER, thus leading to better performance on the LA task.

title: CannyEdit: Selective Canny Control and Dual-Prompt Guidance for Training-free Image Editing
authors: Weiyan Xie, Han Gao, Didan Deng, Kaican Li, Yongxiang Huang, April Hua Liu, Nevin L. Zhang
abstract: Recent text-to-image advances enable training-free regional image editing by leveraging generative priors of foundation models. However, existing methods struggle to balance text adherence in edited regions, context fidelity in unedited areas, and seamless integration of edits. We introduce CannyEdit, a novel training-free framework addressing this trilemma through two key innovations. First, Selective Canny Control uses structural guidance from a Canny ControlNet exclusively on unedited regions, preserving original details while enabling precise, text-driven edits. Second, Dual-Prompt Guidance combines a local prompt for specific edits with a global prompt for overall coherence. This synergy achieves controllable local editing for object addition, replacement, and removal, offering a superior trade-off among text adherence, context fidelity, and seamlessness compared to mask-based methods. Moreover, CannyEdit works effectively with rough masks or single-point hints for addition tasks and integrates seamlessly with vision-language models without training. Extensive evaluations highlight CannyEdit’s strong performance in complex instruction-based editing and object addition scenarios.

title: Range-Level Preloading with Scalable Watch-Time Estimation for Billion-User Streaming Systems
authors: Yucheng Zhang, Zhiwei Fan, Tong Liu, Guanyan Peng, Haodan Zhang, Zhen Wang, Pengjin Xie, Liang Liu, Huadong Ma
abstract: Short-video platforms have grown rapidly by allowing users to browse rich media content through seamless swiping. However, the inherently random nature of swipe behavior creates significant challenges for bandwidth efficiency and playback continuity, often resulting in stalls and unnecessary data transfers. We present OffLoad, a new preloading framework that enhances bandwidth efficiency and playback quality using range-based downloading, which generalizes traditional chunk-based preloading to arbitrary-length segments for finer-grained control. At the core of OffLoad is a two-dimensional watch-time estimation model that jointly captures user preferences and video characteristics. Guided by this estimator, OffLoad introduces a hybrid preloading algorithm that integrates heuristic rules with a learning-based module trained directly on large-scale production data, enabling strong generalization in deployment. Following extensive system-level optimization, OffLoad has been deployed on a commercial short-video platform for more than six months. Our A/B testing results show that OffLoad increases overall user watch-time by 1.1‰, while simultaneously reducing 0.13% rebuffering events and 4.92% of bandwidth consumption.

title: Meteorological data and Sky Images meets Neural Models for Photovoltaic Power Forecasting
authors: Inés Montoya-Espinagosa, Antonio Agudo
abstract: Due to the rise in the use of renewable energies as an alternative to traditional ones, and especially solar energy, there is increasing interest in studying how to address photovoltaic forecasting in the face of the challenge of variability in photovoltaic energy production, using different methodologies. This work develops a hybrid approach for short and long-term forecasting based on two studies with the same purpose. A multimodal approach that combines images of the sky and photovoltaic energy history with meteorological data is proposed. The main goal is to improve the accuracy of ramp event prediction, increase the robustness of forecasts in cloudy conditions, and extend capabilities beyond nowcasting, to support more efficient operation of the power grid and better management of solar variability. Deep neural models are used for both nowcasting and forecasting solutions, incorporating individual and multiple meteorological variables, as well as an analytical solar position. The results demonstrate that the inclusion of meteorological data, particularly the surface long-wave, radiation downwards, and the combination of wind and solar position, significantly improves current predictions in both nowcasting and forecasting tasks, especially on cloudy days. This study highlights the importance of integrating diverse data sources to improve the reliability and interpretability of solar energy prediction models.

title: A Unified Framework for Unbiased Non-Coherent Over-the-Air Computation
authors: Martin Dahl, Zheng Chen, Erik G. Larsson
abstract: Over-the-Air Computation (OAC) enables efficient data aggregation in large-scale distributed systems by exploiting the superposition property of wireless multiple-access channels. In contrast to most existing studies on OAC assuming exact channel state information, we consider non-coherent OAC (NC-OAC) where the channel phase is unknown at the transmitters. A three-step framework for NC-OAC with a mapping between source data and codewords is proposed: 1) Devices encode their data to non-negative codewords; 2) Devices transmit a sequence of symbols with amplitude proportional to their codewords, such that the receiver can estimate the codeword sum. Estimation of the codeword sum is studied under two scenarios of global channel amplitude knowledge: statistical or instantaneous; 3) The estimated codeword sum is decoded to the desired source data sum at the receiver. With the proposed framework, we first study prior work on NC-OAC and map these to the framework. Next, we define and compare the two most commonly (often implicitly) used mappings for NC-OAC: the Affine and the Augmented Affine mappings. Under the constraint of unbiased estimation, we show that with uniformly distributed data and standard channel assumptions, the Augmented Affine mapping exhibits an order of magnitude lower estimation variance than the Affine mapping with both statistical and instantaneous channel knowledge. This result is validated by extensive simulations. Finally, we propose and analyze a new mapping, which demonstrates superior performance over the previous two affine mappings.

title: Mapping the Landscape of DDoS Attack Detection with Machine Learning in SDN
authors: Arti Gupta, Bineet Kumar Joshi
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Device Intelligence, Computing and Communication Technologies (DICCT)
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) threat is among the most harmful cyber vulnerability, addressing the stability and safety of TCP/IP networks. By jamming systems with bulky traffic from varied origins, these attacks unable services and cause severe monetary loss. Traditional defense techniques often fall short as attackers develop more sophisticated methods. Software-Defined Network (SDN) addresses these difficulties by providing a programmable infrastructure that sets apart the control and data plains, enabling versatile network control. In parallel, Machine Learning (ML) has come up as a powerful approach for disclosing and diminishing DDoS attacks. ML models can intelligently observe traffic patterns to examine irregularity and time dependent vulnerabilities. It was recognized that supervised learning algorithms, like decision tree and deep neural networks, classify congestion of data, while unsupervised approaches like classification and autoencoders detect previously unseen attack patterns. Together, SDN and ML offer a reliable, evolving strategy to counter DDoS attacks effectively. In this paper, we conducted two types of reviews Systematic Literature Review (SLR) and Bibliometric Literature Review (BLR) efficiently analyze the work carried about different researchers in this area. We also suggested that a researcher must done BLR before SLR to get the better understanding of the topics. This study is closely connected to SDG 9 as it works toward making modern network systems more secure and reliable. By strengthening protection against cyber threats, it also supports SDG 16. Additionally, it contributes to SDG 8 by helping create stable and secure digital environments that support business activities and economic growth.

title: Triple Active Bridge Converter with High-Frequency Transformer for Grid Integration of Photovoltaic and Storage Systems
authors: Abdullah Almutairi, Mohammed Alharbi
abstract: In multi-port active bridge converters, emphasizing their benefits in integrating solar PV and energy storage systems. These converters offer advantages such as high-power density, improved efficiency through high-frequency operation and zero voltage switching (ZVS), and the elimination of bulky low-frequency transformers. They facilitate efficient power conversion across various input voltages and loads. Additionally, the integration of wide bandgap semiconductor technologies like silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFET in triple active bridge converters enhances efficiency and reduces the size of passive components, making them suitable for compact and efficient power electronic systems in renewable energy applications.

title: Spatio-temporal Modeling Using a Cost-Effective System for Air Quality Monitoring
authors: Manal Karmoude, Brenton Munhungewarwa, Ryan McKenzie, Jude Kong, Bevan Smith, Vongani Chabalala, Donald Ngobeni, Raghav Chandna, Mukesh Kumar, Redouane Benaini, Bruce Mellado
abstract: Ambient air quality remains a major environmental and public-health concern. While traditional reference-grade monitoring stations provide reliable pollutant measurements, their high installation and maintenance costs limit spatial coverage—particularly in low-resource regions. Moreover, these stations generally do not provide future pollutant concentrations. To address these constraints, the South African Consortium for Air Quality Monitoring (SACAQM) has developed a cost-effective air quality monitoring system that integrates cost-effective sensors, advanced IoT technologies, and predictive modeling capabilities. AI r system provides real-time pollutant data across multiple locations in Gauteng province through a centralized dashboard. After calibration against reference-grade stations, the system demonstrated high reliability, highlighting its potential as a practical alternative to traditional monitoring infrastructure. This study presents the first use of spatio-temporal modeling for 24-hour forecasting using data from a cost-effective air-quality monitoring system deployed in Africa. We applied spatio-temporal deep learning models to pollutant data collected from several sites monitored by AI r system, enabling the extraction of both spatial and temporal patterns relevant for forecasting. The results illustrate the feasibility and promise of combining cost-effective sensing networks with spatio-temporal approaches for air-quality forecasting and early warning.

title:
CsPbBr
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Perovskite Quantum Dots and One-Dimensional ZnO nanorods composites applied for Ultraviolet/Visible Broadband photodetectors
authors: Yu-Jhih Chu, Yi-Hsing Liu, Chih-Chiang Yang, Shoou-Jinn Chang, Sheng-Joue Young
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Broadband photodetection covering the ultraviolet and visible spectral regions remains a critical challenge for low-cost optoelectronic devices. In this work, a hybrid photodetector architecture was realized by integrating one-dimensional (1-D) ZnO nanorods (NRs) with CsPbBr3 perovskite quantum dots (PQDs), forming a sensitized heterostructure for enhanced visible-light response. The structural and compositional features of the pristine 1-D ZnO NRs and PQDs-capped NRs samples were systematically examined through electron microscopy, X-ray diffraction, and optical spectroscopies, revealing successful decoration of CsPbBr3 PQDs on the surface of 1-D ZnO NRs. Photoluminescence and absorption analyses demonstrate that the introduction of PQDs effectively extends the optical response of ZnO from the ultraviolet into the visible region. Electrical measurements further show that the PQDs/1-D ZnO NRs heterostructure exhibits markedly improved photoresponse under blue and green illumination compared with unmodified 1-D ZnO NRs, while maintaining low dark current characteristics. This enhancement is attributed to efficient interfacial charge transfer and suppressed carrier recombination enabled by favorable energy-level alignment between CsPbBr3 PQDs and 1-D ZnO NRs. The proposed hybrid structure provides a viable route toward compact, cost-effective, and low-power broadband photodetectors suitable for ultraviolet and visible-light sensing applications.
